questions: Is the process over-invested in its own ceremony (perspective reset)?
Does the QA/remediate cycle burn tokens disproportionately?
How to extend Qor-logic into autonomous QA without heavy user engagement?

findings.claim: Directionally confirmed: ~83% of phases 104-163 harden governance machinery; ~33% first-pass VETO rate; 51% of ledger entries mention remediation workflows
Prose doctrine does not self-enforce: SG-038 recurred 4+ times post-codification (phases 15/17a/19/20/28); only executable checks stick
Seal-fragile test class (badges/freshness/keywords, est. 50-60% of 404-file suite) asserts generated-artifact state rather than behavior; breaks on nearly every seal
14+ MythologIQ repos carry governance artifacts; bicameral PAMA M1-M5 mutation classes and PUBLIC-repo governance mirroring are the top absorbable patterns
Executable lints with allowlists, scheduled drift detection with automatic GH issue lifecycle, risk-stratified coverage, dry-run modes, regression-guard tests named for issues

Deterministic fail-closed policy evaluation with no LLM in the decision loop; reconstructible Decision BOM from observability signals; graduated execution rings; independently validates Merkle-chained audit design
